hints.text: Plate long white + standard EU blue strip left
Bollards **wedge-shaped**: orange rectangular reflector front / white back (Estonia & Latvia differ)
Poles usually **square concrete w/ a diagonal support beam**; trident-style poletops
Chevrons **white w/ red arrows** + a red outline on the sign edge (Estonia/Russia/Ukraine = opposite colours)
Blue km marker at a **45° angle to the road, L-shaped** (Estonia = perpendicular)
Signs = **thin red border + thin white outline**; A-type guardrails w/ **orange reflectors** (Estonia none, Latvia red/white)
Pedestrian sign **3 stripes, low detail** (like RU/UA; Estonia more detailed; Latvia has 5)
**Lithuanian** (Baltic): letters **Ą Ę Į Ų Ė**; street = **gatvė (g.)**; many place names end **-ai**
Bus-stop sign bus has **4 windows** (Latvia = 5; Estonia same as LT); directional signs blue w/ white text
Architecture = light brick + steep corrugated metal roofs; **less forest → more open/agricultural** than the other Baltics
Gen3 **random small blurs** (also Kaliningrad; not EE/LV); black & red Gen4; **Curonian Spit** (SW) = dunes + windswept Baltic pines + red wooden houses
